title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Urbanization is a global trend that has transformed the way we live, (0) with more than half of the world's population now residing in cities. (9) While cities offer economic opportunities and cultural diversity, they also face immense logistical challenges. (10) Such is the scale of modern megacities that providing basic services like water and transport requires constant innovation. (11) Unless urban planners prioritize sustainability, the environmental impact of these massive hubs will become unsustainable. (12) Not only do cities consume vast amounts of energy, but they also produce the majority of global waste. (13) Regardless of these drawbacks, people continue to migrate to urban centers in search of a better life. (14) Not until recently have architects begun to design 'green' buildings that incorporate vertical gardens to improve air quality. (15) Provided that we can integrate technology with nature, the cities of the future might become healthier places to inhabit. (16) Whether or not we can successfully manage this transition will determine the quality of life for future generations. The complexity of urban systems demands a multidisciplinary approach that considers both social and environmental needs. By rethinking our approach to city living, we can create vibrant spaces that benefit everyone while protecting the planet for the years ahead.

example.text: Urbanization is a global trend that has transformed the way we live, (0) with more than half of the world's population now residing in cities.
example.explanation: Preposition used to introduce a phrase that adds information.

questions.explanation: Conjunction used to introduce a contrast.
Determiner used for emphasis followed by an inversion.
Part of the 'Not only... but also' structure.
Part of the fixed linker 'Regardless of'.
Part of the 'Not until' structure requiring inversion ('have architects').
Conjunction meaning 'if' or 'on the condition that'.
